aah: interjection. Indication of amazement or surprise or enthusiasm.
aahed: verb. simple past and past participle of aah
ace: noun. A playing card showing a single pip, typically the highest or lowest ranking card in a game.
aced: verb. simple past and past participle of ace
ache: verb. To suffer pain; to be the source of, or be in, pain, especially continued dull pain; to be distressed.
ached: verb. simple past and past participle of ache
aha: interjection. An exclamation of understanding, realization, invention, or recognition.
ahead: adverb. At or towards the front; in the direction one is facing or moving.
cad: noun. A low-bred, presuming person; a mean, vulgar fellow, especially one that cannot be trusted with a lady.
cade: adjective. abandoned by its mother and reared by hand
cede: verb. To give up; yield to another. [with to]
cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.
chad: noun. Small pieces of paper punched out from the edges of continuous stationery, or from ballot papers, paper tape, punched cards, etc.
dace: noun. A shoal -forming fish of species Leuciscus leuciscus, common to swift rivers in England and Wales and in Europe.
dacha: noun. A Russian villa or summer house in the countryside.
dah: noun. The spoken representation of a dash in radio and telegraph Morse code.
each: determiner. All; every; qualifying a singular noun, indicating all examples of the thing so named seen as individual or separate items
had: verb. simple past and past participle of have
hade: noun. State; order, estate, rank, degree, or quality.
haha: interjection. An onomatopoeic representation of laughter.
head: noun. The part of the body of an animal or human which contains the brain, mouth, and main sense organs.
headache: noun. A pain or ache in the head.
heh: noun. The fifth letter of many Semitic alphabets (Phoenician 𐤄, Aramaic 𐡄, Hebrew ה, Syriac ܗ, Arabic ه, and others).
ach: noun. Any of several species of plants, such as smallage, wild celery, parsley.
ade: noun. A drink made from a fruit, especially a fizzy one.
adh: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adhola.
ahed: verb. simple past and past participle of ah
ahhed: verb. simple past and past participle of ahh
chee: noun. Synonym of mace (“old Chinese unit of weight”).
dae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Duupa.
dha: noun. Alternative form of dah (“Burmese knife”).
ecad: noun. An organism whose form has been affected by its environment.
hed: noun. The headline of a news story.
